Home Latest News About Calvin Upcoming Events Get in touch More Use tab to navigate through the menu items. Calvin Bailey MBE MP Serving Leyton and Wanstead It's an honour to have been elected as your Member of Parliament and to serve the people and communities of Leyton & Wanstead. ​ My team and I are here to support you - so if there's a local issue you wish to speak to us about, please get in touch.​ GET IN TOUCH Stay up-to-date Sign up to my constituent newsletter. First name * Last name * Email * Postcode * Subscribe me to Calvin Bailey MP's newsletter * By ticking this box I consent to the office of Calvin Bailey MP contacting me via the details above. Privacy Policy | Calvin Bailey MP * Submit Calvin's Work in Parliament & the Constituency Campaigns & Local Priorities Whipps Cross Hospital Whipps Cross Hospital urgently needs an upgrade. Redevelopment will create a state-of-the-art facility, support NHS staff, provide new homes, generate jobs, and improve care for our community. Anti-social Behaviour & Crime Persistent antisocial behaviour and criminality profoundly impact our community. We must tackle these issues, support residents facing intimidation, and work together to create safer streets for all. Public Transport Accessibility A lack of step-free access and changes to local bus services (the W12, W13, and W14 routes) affect families’ and vulnerable residents' access to key services like Whipps Cross Hospital and public transport in the area. National Issues Empowering Young People Education lays the groundwork for future success, and access to the arts, sports, and volunteering is crucial. I want everyone in our area to have access to these opportunities. ​ Supporting Veterans We have around 4,000 veterans in our community in need of support to flourish in civilian life. Improving access to healthcare, housing, and employment is essential. ​ Housing London’s housing crisis—driven by a shortage of affordable homes and insecure renting—hurts families and council budgets. Supporting leaseholders and strengthening renters’ rights are essential for safe, affordable housing. Latest News Response to the attack on Jewish Londoners in Golders Green My message to our Jewish community in Leyton and Wanstead following the horrific attack on Jewish Londoners in Golders Green. 2 days ago Marking the Month of the Military Child I was proud to host a reception in Parliament marking the Month of the Military Child, bringing together the Royal British Legion, Never Such Innocence, parliamentarians, and young people from Armed Forces families. National Issues 3 days ago My London Marathon 2026 run for Samaritans A big thank you to everyone who supported my London Marathon run last weekend! Finished in 03:26 and even got my medal from Dawn Butler MP. I ran for the Samaritans, to show support for our wonderful Waltham Forest branch. 4 days ago 1 2 3 4 5 READ MORE FIND OUT NOW About Calvin Calvin is the Labour Member of Parliament for Leyton and Wanstead, elected on the 5th of July 2024. Born in Zambia and raised in London, his commitment to social justice was shaped by personal experiences, including the tragic murder of his family friend, Stephen Lawrence. Prior to his political career, Bailey served for 24 years in the Royal Air Force, achieving the rank of Wing Commander and earning an MBE for his work. READ MORE How can we help? My team and I regularly hold Advice Surgeries to help people across Leyton & Wanstead. ​ Get in touch today to book a slot at one of our upcoming surgeries in your area: GET IN TOUCH Advice calvin.bailey.mp@parliament.uk ​ 0207 219 7417 Email: ​ Phone: ​ How I Can Help Follow Calvin on Social Media: GET IN TOUCH Calvin is Chair of the All Party Parliamentary Group for the Armed Forces Community. FIND OUT MORE Privacy Policy

pages.title: About Calvin | Calvin Bailey MP
pages.meta_description: Calvin is the Labour MP for Leyton and Wanstead, elected on the 5th of July 2024. Born in Zambia, Calvin’s family moved to London, settling in Plumstead. Growing up in south-east London as a young Black man, he faced far-right violence and was deeply affected by the tragic murder of Stephen Lawrence, the older brother of his close friend Stuart. This event profoundly influenced Calvin’s politics and desire to serve the community.

pages.text_excerpt: --> About Calvin | Calvin Bailey MP top of page Home Latest News About Calvin Upcoming Events Get in touch More Use tab to navigate through the menu items. ABOUT CALVIN Calvin is the Labour MP for Leyton and Wanstead, elected on the 5th of July 2024. Born in Zambia, Calvin’s family moved to London, settling in Plumstead. Growing up in south-east London as a young Black man, he faced far-right violence and was deeply affected by the tragic murder of Stephen Lawrence, the older brother of his close friend Stuart. This event profoundly influenced Calvin’s politics and desire to serve the community. Calvin graduated with a Master of Engineering from the University of Exeter in 1999 and later completed a Master of Arts in War Studies at King's College London in 2017. Calvin spent over 24-years in the Royal Air Force, rising to the rank of Wing Commander. During his military service, he led humanitarian efforts and operations in conflict zones. He was awarded an MBE for his support of relief operations in Haiti and the Philippines. Calvin also proudly led critical missions, including commanding the UK's largest deployed Air Wing during the COVID-19 pandemic and overseeing the evacuation of 14,000 people from Kabul during Operation Pitting where he made the critical decision to double the number of passengers taken per flight, recognising that the risk of overloading the aircraft was outweighed by the risk to the lives of those who would have been left behind. Calvin’s transition to politics was inspired by his desire to effect change and advocate for working-class communities. He is a graduate of Labour's young leaders programme and the Franco-British Council. On July 24, 2024, Calvin made his maiden speech during a debate on the Global Combat Air Programme. Since entering Parliament, he has been active in various roles. Calvin serves on the Defence Select Committee and actively takes part in several All-Party Parliamentary Groups (APPGs). Calvin is also a dedicated champion for the Labour African Network and is committed to championing diversity, supporting veterans, empowering young people, and serving the people of Leyton and Wanstead. NEED HELP WITH A LOCAL ISSUE? My team and I are here to help in any way we can. Click the button below to raise an issue with us. GET IN TOUCH calvin.bailey.mp@parliament.uk ​ 0207 219 7417 Email: ​ Phone: Follow Calvin on Social Media: GET IN TOUCH Calvin is Chair of the All Party Parliamentary Group for the Armed Forces Community. h2: Calvin is Chair of the All Party Parliamentary Group for the Armed Forces Community.
pages.text_excerpt: --> ON THE ISSUES | Calvin Bailey MP top of page Home Latest News About Calvin Upcoming Events Get in touch More Use tab to navigate through the menu items. The War in the Middle East Over the past months, the horrifying accounts of the crisis in Gaza, the West Bank have left many of us in shock. I have heard moving testimonies from constituents with loved ones in danger, and like you, I am deeply troubled by the brutal impact of this conflict. As your Member of Parliament, I am committed to representing your views and influencing government actions to bring this situation to an end. The urgent plight of civilians, especially children, cannot be ignored; we must act now to stop the violence, agree to a ceasefire, secure the release of all hostages, and work towards a lasting peace that has been needed for so long. My Urgent Call for Action I am advocating for an immediate ceasefire in Palestine. The suffering of civilians, especially children, must end now. I'm pushing for the release of all hostages as a critical step towards de-escalation. In the long term we must have a two-state solution that ensures both a secure Israel and a viable Palestinian state. My Diplomatic Engagement I have been actively representing your concerns through various diplomatic channels. Regular meetings with Middle East Minister Hamish Falconer MP have allowed me to communicate the strength of feeling on these critical issues. I've engaged with key regional figures, most notably Palestinian Authority Ambassador Husam Zomlot, with whom I've developed a close understanding. Our discussions on October 22nd focused on enhancing UK support for international law. These ongoing dialogues are crucial in our efforts to push for peace and a just resolution to the conflict. My Support for International Law I firmly believe that all conflicts must be governed by international law. As such, I strongly support institutions like the International Criminal Court (ICC) and the International Court of Justice (ICJ) in their role to determine and uphold these laws. Our government has taken a clear stance on this matter, dropping the previous administration's attempt to intervene in the ICC's process regarding potential arrest warrants for Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu over alleged war crimes in Gaza. We've also affirmed our belief that the ICC has jurisdiction over Gaza. We've implemented sanctions against individuals and entities responsible for human rights abuses against Palestinian communities in the West Bank, demonstrating our commitment to upholding international law and condemning illegal settlements. I will continue to support our government's efforts to hold all parties accountable and advocate for strict adherence to international law in this conflict and beyond. Arms Export Review On September 2nd, our government took a significant step by conducting a thorough review of UK arms exports to Israel. This rigorous process, aligned with our legal obligations, concluded that there is a clear risk that military exports to Israel, if used in Gaza operations, might contribute to serious violations of international humanitarian law. As a result, we've suspended all licenses for exports to Israel for items that could potentially be used in the current conflict, whether in Gaza, the West Bank, or Lebanon. I've personally requested ongoing reviews of arms licenses and raised specific concerns about the international supply chain for F-35s. Government Actions Since July, the UK Government has taken a number of steps to address the conflict. On July 14th, Foreign Secretary David Lammy visited Israel and the West Bank, calling for an immediate ceasefire and a two-state solution. On September 2nd, the government suspended arms export licenses to Israel after determining a clear risk of serious violations of international humanitarian law. pages.title: HOW I CAN HELP | Calvin Bailey MP
headings: h2: USEFUL CONTACTS:
h2: STILL NEED SUPPORT?
h2: Calvin is Chair of the All Party Parliamentary Group for the Armed Forces Community.
pages.text_excerpt: --> HOW I CAN HELP | Calvin Bailey MP top of page Home Latest News About Calvin Upcoming Events Get in touch More Use tab to navigate through the menu items. Home Latest News About Calvin Upcoming Events Get in touch More Use tab to navigate through the menu items. HOW I CAN HELP: As your MP, I can help with matters for which central government is directly responsible. Problems with local authority services, such as waste collection, road maintenance, planning, and school and housing allocation are managed by either Waltham Forest Council or Redbridge Council , depending on where you live. These enquiries should be directed to your local councillors first. ​ If you live in Waltham Forest, you can find your councillors’ details here , and if you live in Redbridge, you can find your councillors’ details here. Although I have no authority on Council decisions, and I would encourage you to contact your local councillors first, I am happy to write to the Council and ask them to look into a problem or reconsider a particular issue. I am unable to intervene in private disputes with neighbours or employers; with companies who have sold you faulty goods, nor can I help to settle family arguments. Furthermore, I cannot interfere with court decisions or offer legal advice, nor can my team. If you believe you need a surgery appointment, please email us. My team includes specialised staff who are often best placed to assist with personal enquiries, so a meeting with me may not always be the most effective way to resolve your issue. However, if appropriate, my team will arrange an appointment for you. If you are not a Leyton and Wanstead constituent: Parliamentary protocol means MPs may only take up issues on behalf of constituents. If you are not a constituent, I cannot act on your behalf. You can check your constituency at http://www.parliament.uk/mps-lords-and-offices/mps/ USEFUL CONTACTS: If you are experiencing an emergency, or need to reach another organisation for help or advice, please find below a list of useful contacts and support services. ​ ​ Police - Emergency: 999 & Non-Emergency: 101 NHS: 111 NELFT Mental Health Direct : 0800 995 1000 Crime Stoppers : 0800 555 111 Samaritans : 116 123 Shelter : 0808 800 4444 (homelessness) Centrepoint : 0808 800 0661 (youth homelessness and housing) Stepchange : 0800 138 1111 (debt advice) Freephone 24-Hour National Domestic Abuse Helpline: 0808 2000 247 Solace Advice and Advocacy Waltham Forest : 073 4068 3382 (domestic abuse and sexual violence) Solace Advice and Advocacy Redbridge : 0800 145 6410 Law Society: 020 7320 5650 (find a solicitor service) Citizens Advice Waltham Forest : 0300 330 1175 Citizens Advice Redbridge : 0800 144 8 444 Healthwatch (national number) : 03000 683 000 (NHS and social care complaints) ​​ ​ STILL NEED SUPPORT? GET IN TOUCH calvin.bailey.mp@parliament.uk ​ 0207 219 7417 Email: ​ Phone: Follow Calvin on Social Media: GET IN TOUCH Calvin is Chair of the All Party Parliamentary Group for the Armed Forces Community.
